testcase.name: test_handle_errors_client_error_with_exception_text
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_http.HTTPTest testMethod=test_handle_errors_client_error_with_exception_text>
def test_handle_errors_client_error_with_exception_text(self): with self.assertRaises(ClientError): status_code = 400 text = """ <ExceptionReport> <ExceptionText>Mock error</ExceptionText> <ExceptionText>Something else which is not used here </ExceptionText> </ExceptionReport> """ Response = namedtuple("Response", ['status_code', 'text']) mock_response = Response(status_code=status_code, text=text) > http._handle_errors(mock_response)
test/test_http.py:29: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def _handle_errors(response: urllib3.response.HTTPResponse): """Handle error responses from FMI service""" > if 400 <= response.status < 500: ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: 'Response' object has no attribute 'status'
fmi_weather_client/http.py:144: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_async_get_forecast_by_coordinates

failure.message: RuntimeError: There is no current event loop in thread 'MainThread'.

testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_init.FMIWeatherTest testMethod=test_async_get_forecast_by_coordinates> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4347894416'>
@mock.patch('requests.get', side_effect=test_data.mock_coordinate_forecast_response) def test_async_get_forecast_by_coordinates(self, mock_get): > loop = asyncio.get_event_loop() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test/test_init.py:54: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <asyncio.unix_events._UnixDefaultEventLoopPolicy object at 0x103279e80>
def get_event_loop(self): """Get the event loop for the current context.
Returns an instance of EventLoop or raises an exception. """ if self._local._loop is None: > raise RuntimeError('There is no current event loop in thread %r.' % threading.current_thread().name) E RuntimeError: There is no current event loop in thread 'MainThread'.
/opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/asyncio/events.py:715: RuntimeError

testcase.name: test_get_forecast_by_coordinates
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_init.FMIWeatherTest testMethod=test_get_forecast_by_coordinates> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4347896432'>
@mock.patch('requests.get', side_effect=test_data.mock_coordinate_forecast_response) def test_get_forecast_by_coordinates(self, mock_get): > forecast = fmi_weather_client.forecast_by_coordinates(29.742731, 67.583988) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test/test_init.py:49: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fmi_weather_client/__init__.py:97: in forecast_by_coordinates response = http.request_forecast_by_coordinates(lat, lon, timestep_hours) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:54: in request_forecast_by_coordinates return _send_request(params) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:137: in _send_request _handle_errors(response) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
response = <urllib3.response.HTTPResponse object at 0x10332dae0>
def _handle_errors(response: urllib3.response.HTTPResponse): """Handle error responses from FMI service""" if 400 <= response.status < 500: data = xmltodict.parse(response.data.decode('utf-8')) try: error_message = data['ExceptionReport']['Exception']['ExceptionText'][0] > raise ClientError(response.status, error_message) E fmi_weather_client.errors.ClientError
fmi_weather_client/http.py:148: ClientError

@mock.patch('requests.get', side_effect=test_data.mock_server_error_response) def test_server_error_response(self, mock_get): with self.assertRaises(ServerError): > fmi_weather_client.weather_by_coordinates(27.31317, 63.14343)
test/test_init.py:94: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fmi_weather_client/__init__.py:18: in weather_by_coordinates response = http.request_weather_by_coordinates(lat, lon) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:29: in request_weather_by_coordinates return _send_request(params) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:137: in _send_request _handle_errors(response) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def _handle_errors(response: urllib3.response.HTTPResponse): """Handle error responses from FMI service""" if 400 <= response.status < 500: data = xmltodict.parse(response.data.decode('utf-8')) try: error_message = data['ExceptionReport']['Exception']['ExceptionText'][0] > raise ClientError(response.status, error_message) E fmi_weather_client.errors.ClientError
fmi_weather_client/http.py:148: ClientError
